examples:
<sqf>
private _hashmap1 = ["cow", "cat", "chicken"] createHashMapFromArray [100, 200, 200];
private _hashmap2 = ["cow", "cat", "chicken", "camel"] createHashMapFromArray [150, 300, 400, 800];
_hashmap1 merge _hashmap2; // _hashmap is [["cow",100],["cat",200],["chicken",200],["camel",800]]
</sqf>

<sqf>
private _hashmap1 = ["cow", "cat", "chicken"] createHashMapFromArray [100, 200, 200];
private _hashmap2 = ["cow", "cat", "chicken", "camel"] createHashMapFromArray [150, 300, 400, 800];
_hashmap1 merge [_hashmap2, true]; // _hashmap is [["cow",150],["cat",300],["chicken",400],["camel",800]]
</sqf>

<sqf>
private _hashmap1 = ["cow", "cat", "chicken"] createHashMapFromArray [100, 200, 200];
private _hashmap2 = ["cow", "cat", "chicken", "camel"] createHashMapFromArray [150, 300, 400, 800];
_hashmap1 merge [_hashmap2, false]; // _hashmap is [["cow",100],["cat",200],["chicken",200],["camel",800]] as Syntax 1
</sqf>
name: merge

Item.desc: all key-value pairs from this HashMap are added to ''hashMap1''. If a key from ''hashMap2'' already exists in ''hashMap1'', it doesn't overwrite the value in ''hashMap1''.
Item.name: hashMap2

Item.desc: all key-value pairs from this HashMap are added to ''hashMap1''
Item.name: hashMap2

Item.desc: if [[true]], keys from ''hashMap1'' that also exist in ''hashMap2'' are overwritten. When set to [[true]], merging is faster
Item.name: overwriteExisting
